page_summary: Page 1: Assembly instructions for the Laminate Activity Table with standard height adjustable legs or height adjustable short legs. Page 2: This page shows how to assemble a table top with silver plates, legs, and a support bar using screws and an Allen key. Page 3: This page shows adjustable legs installation, inserting leg plates into the silver plate and tightening the screws.

instruction_steps.text: Insert leg plate into the silver plate, tighten the screws.

inventory.inferred_function: Serves as the horizontal top surface of a table, forming the primary work/display surface and connecting with supporting components to complete the table assembly.
inventory.function_rationale: The steps show TABLE TOP appearing at STEP 1 alongside SILVER PLATE (likely a decorative/auxiliary component on the top) and again at STEP 4 with SUPPORT BAR, indicating it is the upper surface that the rest of the structure (legs via the support bar) integrates with to form the table. This usage aligns with a role as the table's top surface.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: Rounded-rectangle, thin, flat top surface serving as the table's upper work surface.
inventory.surface_texture: flat, smooth top surface; slightly curved corners
orientation_cues: no visible mounting features on the visible face
distinctive_landmarks: rounded-rectangle top with smooth edge, slightly curved corners, flat top surface

differences: TABLE SURFACE may have visible holes or mounting features; TABLE TOP shown here as a flat rounded-rectangle with no visible holes in the visible face
TOP PLATE is typically decorative/auxiliary on the top; TABLE TOP is the primary upper surface that interfaces with supports as the main surface
WORK SURFACE is a more generic term; TABLE TOP is specifically the upper structural surface of the table in this assembly
TABLETOP is another spelling variant; physically corresponds to the same part type but may differ in labeling; function and geometry here correspond to TABLE TOP

action_relevant_notes: Used in STEP 1 and STEP 4 as the table's horizontal top surface; tactile identification relies on flat, smooth, rounded-rectangle plate without visible holes on the face.
Refer to its symmetry and rounded corners to distinguish from plates with mounting holes or beveled edges.
Uncertainties: exact thickness not measurable from the crop; possible subtle bevels or rim around edge not clearly defined.
uncertainties: Exact thickness is not measurable from the crop.
Possible subtle bevels or rim around edge not clearly defined.

inventory.inferred_function: Acts as a mounting/connector plate that attaches the table leg to the tabletop, providing a surface to secure the leg to the table assembly.
inventory.function_rationale: Step 1 uses the silver plate with the table top, suggesting initial attachment to the top. Step 2 shows the silver plate paired with a leg, indicating it serves as an intermediary fastening surface between the table top and the leg to establish a stable support for the structure.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A thin, solid rounded-rectangle plate with a flat top and rounded corners that serves as an intermediary mounting surface between the table top and the leg (hole-free, smooth edge).
inventory.size_relative: large rectangular piece occupying substantial area of the drawing area

differences: has holes or angular features
not flat and featureless on the top surface
has holes or notches and angular geometry
not a smooth hole-free sheet
likely designed with mounting holes or different thickness/edges
not a uniform rounded rectangle hole-free sheet

action_relevant_notes: Acts as an intermediary fastening surface between tabletop and leg; handle by aligning with leg and top fasteners.
Identify by smooth, hole-free surface and rounded corners to distinguish from bracket/plate types with holes or angular features.
uncertainties: exact thickness cannot be measured from the image
whether there are subtle perforations or markings not visible in the crop

inventory.inferred_function: Provides vertical support in the table assembly and connects to other structural parts (support bar and, ultimately, the tabletop) to form the table frame.
inventory.function_rationale: The leg appears in steps where it is paired with the support bar (step 3) and in steps involving the table top (step 2 shows the leg with the silver plate and step 4 pairs the support bar with the table top, implying the leg contributes to the table’s frame alongside these parts). This suggests the leg’s role is to support and stabilize the tabletop via connection to the support bar.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: Vertical cylindrical leg with an angled brace and a flat base plate that mounts to the frame, providing upright support for the table.
inventory.shape: vertical cylindrical leg with an angled brace and a flat base plate
inventory.size_relative: tall and narrow compared to base plate width
inventory.thickness_or_depth: thin-walled leg tube; brace and base plate have similar thickness
inventory.edge_profile: leg edge is smooth cylindrical; base plate edges are flat rectangular
inventory.surface_texture: leg surface smooth; base plate likely flat/smooth
protrusions: angled brace acts as a structural protrusion; base plate may have a raised edge or tab at mounting interface (unclear)
grooves_slots_holes: base plate contains multiple circular holes arranged along its length; additional fastener holes may exist on leg/bracket interface
inventory.symmetry: overall asymmetric due to brace orientation; single upright member with one side brace
orientation_cues: top of leg aligns with vertical tube; base plate lies horizontal
distinctive_landmarks: gusset/brace on one side; base plate with holes

differences: LEG is vertical and tubular with a curved/angled brace; SUPPORT BAR is flat elongated and lacks a vertical tube
TABLE TOP is flat rectangular and horizontal; LEG is tall, cylindrical, and vertically oriented with an angled brace

action_relevant_notes: Used in steps 2 and 3; connects to support bar and tabletops via base plate holes for fasteners; brace orientation helps locate proper side for assembly.
uncertainties: exact hole count on base plate not clearly legible
thickness of leg wall and base plate not precisely measurable from image
position and number of mounting holes on leg-brace interface not clearly visible

inventory.inferred_function: Acts as a brace that connects the table leg to the table top, providing structural support and stability to the table (reduces wobble and maintains spacing between components).
inventory.function_rationale: In Step 3 it is paired with the LEG, and in Step 4 it is paired with the TABLE TOP, indicating it serves as a bridging member between the leg and top to reinforce the table’s frame.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A small, elbow-shaped, thin bar that serves as a bridging brace between leg and top to stabilize the table.
inventory.shape: L-shaped bar with two segments forming an elbow
inventory.size_relative: small fragment visible; length and full size uncertain
inventory.edge_profile: rounded outer edges; single bend at elbow
grooves_slots_holes: no holes or slots visible in this crop
inventory.symmetry: no clear symmetry observed in the cropped portion
distinctive_landmarks: corner elbow bend; two flat faces on each leg forming an L shape

differences: this part is an L-shaped bar with a single elbow and no visible holes in the observed crop; other braces may be straight or have different hole patterns.
stiffeners are typically flatter and may lie flush along an edge; this part has a distinct elbow and two-segment form.
beams are usually longer and span between supports; this is a small, elbowed fragment likely serving as a joint connector.
leg brackets often have attachment holes or wider mounting surfaces for leg-to-top attachment; this observed portion shows no holes and a curved elbow that suggests a different mounting role.

action_relevant_notes: Used in steps 3 and 4 to connect leg to top as a stabilizing bridge.
uncertainties: Only a corner of the support bar is visible in this crop; features such as hole pattern, total length, and exact bend geometry are not fully observable

inventory.inferred_function: provides a leg support that is attached to the silver plate to form or extend a leg assembly, secured with screws
inventory.function_rationale: The instruction says to insert the leg plate into the silver plate and tighten the screws, indicating it acts as a structural member that becomes part of a leg assembly when fastened.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A flat or slightly flat-edged plate that mates with the silver plate to extend a leg assembly when screws are tightened; geometry is not clearly visible in the current crop, but its role is to become part of the leg structure.
inventory.shape: Not fully visible in the provided crop; the leg plate boundary is not clearly shown, with only the edge of a vertical leg and a nearby flat plate region partially seen.
inventory.size_relative: Unclear due to partial view; exact dimensions cannot be determined from this crop.
inventory.edge_profile: Edge of the leg plate boundary is not clearly shown in the crop; an edge alongside a vertical leg is visible
inventory.surface_texture: Partial view shows a flat base/plate region near the leg; texture details cannot be confirmed from this crop

grooves_slots_holes: none observed in the current crop
inventory.symmetry: Not observable in crop; symmetry cannot be determined
orientation_cues: likely oriented to align with the vertical leg and the silver plate for connection
no explicit markers visible in this crop
distinctive_landmarks: edge near the vertical cylindrical leg and adjacent flat plate area (from partial view)

differences: foot plate would lie flat on a surface and interface with the leg plate from beneath or forward-facing; the leg plate is described as part of a leg assembly rather to be a floor contact piece, though the current crop does not show full geometry to confirm surface orientation
brackets often feature bends or mounting tabs; leg plate is described as a structural member that inserts into a silver plate and is secured with screws rather than a bent support with tabs
support plate typically provides intermediate support surface; leg plate is specifically described as part of a leg assembly and is inserted into the silver plate and screwed in
leg bracket would usually include mounting points or shapes to attach to a leg assembly; leg plate is a mating, insertable member that becomes part of the leg assembly when fastened
base plate serves as a foundation plane; leg plate is a member that extends a leg assembly and attaches to a silver plate rather than being the base itself

action_relevant_notes: Insert leg plate into silver plate and tighten screws to secure.
In this crop, leg plate geometry is not clearly visible; rely on tactile feel for a flat plate with potential screw holes as part of the leg connection.
If screws are present, locate corresponding screw holes on the silver plate to guide alignment.
Be mindful of the adjacent vertical leg edge when identifying the boundary of the leg plate in tactile exploration.
uncertainties: Leg plate geometry and boundary are not clearly depicted in the provided crop; identification relies on function description and proximity to the silver plate and leg rather than clear shape cues.

inventory.inferred_function: serves as a receptacle/anchoring plate to attach the leg plate to the assembly using screws
inventory.function_rationale: The instruction specifies inserting the leg plate into the silver plate and securing with screws, indicating the silver plate provides a mounting surface and fastening point for the leg plate.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A thin, elongated mounting edge with threaded screw holes used to anchor the leg plate in place.
inventory.shape: elongated rectangular plate with a pair of slender vertical elements in the observed crop; overall tall and narrow
inventory.size_relative: tall and narrow relative to its width
inventory.edge_profile: straight side edges with curved/top rounded corners; bottom edge flat
inventory.surface_texture: flat faces along edges; smooth surface

focal feature is an edge/edge-row of holes rather than a broad mounting surface
less likely to show multiple circular holes along a base edge; this part emphasizes an anchoring edge for leg attachment

action_relevant_notes: Use tactile locating of screw holes to align leg plate; feel for the edge curvature and the base-edge hole row to confirm orientation and position
if holes are not felt, inspect for subtle edge recesses or countersinks along the base edge
uncertainties: Only partial view is shown; exact hole count/positions and full shape may vary across the complete part; some holes/slots may not be resolvable in this crop

inventory.function_rationale: In step 1, screws are tightened after inserting the leg plate into the silver plate, indicating their role is to fasten these parts together and provide structural connection.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: Small threaded fasteners with a sharp tip and a recessed head used to secure leg plate to the silver plate.
inventory.shape: cylindrical shaft with a small head on one end and a pointed tip on the other
inventory.size_relative: much smaller than the leg/plate thickness
inventory.edge_profile: sharp pointed tip at the end
inventory.surface_texture: threaded shaft visible along length; head may have a tactile recess
grooves_slots_holes: head recess (likely Philips or slotted) tactilely detectable; threads along the shaft; surrounding plates have holes for screw heads but these screws themselves are threaded

orientation_cues: tip at opposite end of head

differences: larger overall size
hex or slotted bolt heads not recess-focused

not a nuts; lacks interior threading

no internal threads to engage with

permanent fastener not designed to be rotated into place
generally smooth shaft without threading

action_relevant_notes: To use, insert screw into aligned holes in leg plate and silver plate, then tighten with appropriate screwdriver until seated; feel for shaft threads engaging and head recess aligning with driver tip

page_summaries.summary: Assembly instructions for the Laminate Activity Table with standard height adjustable legs or height adjustable short legs.
This page shows how to assemble a table top with silver plates, legs, and a support bar using screws and an Allen key.
This page shows adjustable legs installation, inserting leg plates into the silver plate and tightening the screws.
